events.title: US Warships Transit Strait of Hormuz for Mine Clearing
events.desc: Two US Navy warships transited the Strait of Hormuz to begin clearing Iranian-laid mines, a move confirmed by US Central Command. Tehran denied the claims, with the Revolutionary Guards threatening severe action against military vessels in the strategic waterway. This transit follows President Trump's announcement that the US had begun 'clearing out' the strait.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Kill at Least 18 in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: The Lebanese Health Ministry reported that at least 18 people were killed in Israeli airstrikes across southern Lebanon. The attacks hit various locations, including Beirut's southern suburbs and the eastern Bekaa Valley, occurring hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced. This escalation highlights the volatile situation in the region despite ongoing diplomatic efforts.

events.title: Israeli Strike Kills at Least Six at Gaza Police Checkpoint
events.desc: An Israeli airstrike targeted a police checkpoint in the Bureij camp in the central Gaza Strip, resulting in at least six fatalities and several injuries. The Israeli military has not yet commented on the incident. This attack occurred amidst ongoing conflict and tensions in the region.

events.title: Iran's Asymmetric Counterair Campaign Continues
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles have continued to target regional military installations. Recent attacks include strikes on Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, damaging critical assets. These actions are part of Iran's ongoing asymmetric strategy aimed at degrading adversary air capabilities.

events.title: US Deploys Mines over Iranian Village
events.desc: Evidence suggests the US deployed Gator Scatterable Mine systems over Kafari, a village near Shiraz, Iran, resulting in fatalities. Experts identified the munitions as US-made anti-tank mines, indicating a significant escalation in the conflict.

events.title: Iranian Strikes on IRGC Compound and School
events.desc: New videos corroborate strikes on an IRGC compound and an adjacent school in Minab, Iran. The attacks, involving multiple missiles, highlight the ongoing kinetic exchanges and their impact on civilian areas.

events.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Conclude with Disagreements
events.desc: Negotiations between the United States and Iran in Islamabad have temporarily paused, with significant disagreements remaining. The talks aimed to end the six-week war, with the Strait of Hormuz reportedly being a key point of contention. Both sides are presenting different peace proposals, indicating a complex path forward.

events.title: Pope Leo Urges World Leaders to Reject War
events.desc: Pope Leo XIV has called on world leaders to abandon war as a means of conflict resolution and to pursue peace. This plea comes amidst ongoing diplomatic efforts and the continuing conflict in the Middle East.

events.title: France and Turkey Discuss Ceasefire and Ukraine
events.desc: French President Emmanuel Macron engaged in discussions with Turkish President Tayyip Erdogan, covering the ongoing Iran ceasefire negotiations and the situation in Ukraine. This diplomatic engagement highlights efforts to manage multiple international crises.

events.title: Iranian Internet Blackout During Strikes
events.desc: During recent Israeli and American strikes, Iran experienced a near-total internet blackout, with traffic collapsing by 98 percent. This deliberate shutdown of communications infrastructure by the Iranian government aimed to control information flow during military operations.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and their associated passwords have been leaked online. The breach affects 12 out of 13 ministries, potentially exposing sensitive information and highlighting significant cybersecurity vulnerabilities.

events.title: Israeli Strikes in Lebanon Escalate Amidst Ceasefire Concerns
events.desc: Despite a US-Iran ceasefire announcement, Israel has conducted extensive strikes across southern Lebanon, causing significant casualties. Lebanon states it is not included in the US-Iran ceasefire, indicating a complex and fragmented regional security landscape.

events.title: Pro-Palestinian Protest Leads to Mass Arrests in London
events.desc: Over 200 individuals were arrested at a pro-Palestinian protest in London's Trafalgar Square. The demonstration opposed the UK's ban on the Palestine Action group, with protesters deemed liable for arrest for their support of the banned organization.

events.title: Lebanon's Beirut Airport Remains Operational Amidst Bombing
events.desc: Beirut airport, Lebanon's sole international passenger facility, continues to operate flights despite ongoing Israeli bombing campaigns. Smoke from recent strikes was visible, but the airport remains functional, albeit with numerous cancellations.
